questions: What is the current test health of the Go codebase? Run all tests and categorize failures by severity (compilation errors, runtime panics, assertion failures)
Identify all TODO/FIXME comments in Go code and assess which represent critical technical debt vs minor improvements
Locate any remaining unsafe json_ok call patterns that need hardening with safe escaping
Find hardcoded paths in Go code that should use path utilities from pkg/storage/paths.go
Check error handling completeness - identify unhandled errors, panic risks, and nil pointer dereference risks
Compare shell utility subcommands in .aether/aether-utils.sh to their Go equivalents and identify parity gaps
Cross-reference CLAUDE.md architecture claims with actual Go implementation to find stale or incorrect documentation
Review goroutine usage for leak risks, missing WaitGroups, and unbuffered channel issues
Check resource management for file handle leaks, unclosed files, and defer statement coverage
Assess file I/O patterns for race conditions and concurrent access safety
